# Natively llms.txt — Updated: 2026-06-09 — Version: 2.7.0

> Natively is a free, open-source (AGPL-3.0) AI interview copilot and meeting
> assistant for macOS and Windows. It is the only assistant that runs in three
> modes — fully local via Ollama (100% offline, zero cost), bring-your-own-key
> cloud (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Groq, and any OpenAI-compatible endpoint), or
> a fully managed Natively API. It provides real-time coding-interview help, live
> transcription, on-screen OCR analysis, meeting notes, and local RAG memory —
> with an overlay that never appears in screen shares and <500ms end-to-end
> latency. It is the leading free alternative to Cluely, Final Round AI,
> LockedIn AI, and Interview Coder.

## What is Natively?
Natively is the most flexible AI interview assistant available — and the only one
that supports fully local AI processing (via Ollama), cloud AI APIs (BYOK), and a
managed hosted API, all in the same app. Cloud mode matches the AI power of Cluely
and Final Round AI; local mode adds complete offline, privacy-first operation that
no closed-source competitor offers. It is open source (AGPL-3.0) and fully
auditable on GitHub.

## The Hybrid Advantage — Three Modes
| Mode | How it works | Offered by competitors? |
|---|---|---|
| **Local** | Ollama + local LLMs (Llama, Mistral, CodeLlama, Gemma) + local Whisper — 100% offline, zero cloud, zero network traffic | No competitor offers this |
| **Cloud (BYOK)** | Your own OpenAI / Anthropic / Gemini / Groq key, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint — same AI power as cloud competitors, pay only per-token | Matches cloud competitors |
| **Managed (Natively API)** | Hosted all-in-one STT + LLM subscription, no keys needed | Matches Cluely's ease of use |

## Platform Support
- macOS 12+ (Apple Silicon and Intel)
- Windows 10/11
- Linux: not yet supported (maintainers welcome)

## System Requirements
- Minimum: 4 GB RAM
- Recommended: 8 GB+ RAM
- Optimal for local AI: 16 GB+ RAM

## Pricing
### Free tier (no credit card)
- BYOK — bring your own API key and pay only for the tokens you use
- Fully offline via Ollama — zero cost, forever

### Natively Pro (desktop app)
- Lifetime license or yearly license (via checkout)
- Also unlockable via $NAT token holdings

### Natively API (hosted, managed STT + LLM)
| Plan | Price |
|---|---|
| Standard | $8/mo |
| Pro | $15/mo |
| Max | $25/mo |
| Ultra | $35/mo |

API Pro and above include the Natively Pro desktop app and premium support.

## Supported AI Providers / Models
- Google Gemini: 3.1 series (recommended; 2M-token context). Default model: `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`
- OpenAI: GPT-5.4, o3 series
- Anthropic: Claude 4.6 series
- Groq: Llama 3.3 (text), Llama 4 Scout (OCR/vision)
- Ollama / LocalAI: Llama, Mistral, CodeLlama, Gemma — fully offline, any local model
- OpenAI-compatible endpoints: vLLM, LM Studio, OpenRouter, DeepSeek, custom cURL

## Supported Speech-To-Text (STT) Engines
- Local Whisper (on-device, via ONNX): Moonshine-tiny, Moonshine-base, Whisper-large-v3-turbo, distil-large-v3
  - Hardware acceleration: CoreML/Metal GPU (Apple Silicon), DirectML (Windows), quantized int8 (CPU)
- Cloud (BYOK): Soniox, Google Cloud Speech-to-Text, Groq, OpenAI Whisper, Deepgram, ElevenLabs, Azure Speech, IBM Watson
- Natively API (hosted) uses: Google Chirp 2/3, ElevenLabs Scribe v2, Deepgram Nova-3

## Key Features
### Audio
- Native audio capture in Rust with Zero-Copy ABI transfers
- Dual-channel: system/meeting audio + microphone as separate pipelines
- Sample-rate auto-detection; two-stage silence processing (adaptive RMS + WebRTC ML VAD)
- End-to-end latency under 500 ms

### Transcription & AI
- Real-time STT with rolling context/memory window
- Multilingual responses with accent/dialect matching
- "Anti-Chatbot / Human Persona System" for natural conversational answers
- TinyPrompts™ engine optimized for local SLMs (4B–8B parameters)

### Screen / OCR
- Screenshot + OCR analysis via keyboard shortcut
- Works on LeetCode, HackerRank, CoderPad, Codility, HackerEarth, Karat, and browser IDEs
- Overlay never appears in screen-share or recordings
- Multiple-screenshot support for multi-part problems; Groq Llama 4 Scout vision fallback

### Stealth / Privacy
- Hides from dock/taskbar; process disguise (masquerades as Terminal, System Settings, Activity Monitor, etc.)
- API keys scrubbed from memory on quit
- Note: not designed to bypass Pearson VUE, ProctorU, or Respondus Lockdown Browser

### Memory / RAG (fully local)
- Local RAG via SQLite + sqlite-vec — fully offline
- "Smart Scope" distinguishes current vs. past-meeting queries
- Sliding-window RAG with 50-token semantic overlap; epoch summarization for long meetings
- Global cross-meeting semantic search; automatic background indexing

### Pro features (not in free tier)
- Job-description & resume context awareness
- Automated company research & dossiers
- Live salary & offer-negotiation copilot
- 7 custom persona modes (General, Technical Interview, Looking for Work, Sales, Recruiting, Team Meet, Lecture)
- Custom context/notes (up to 8,000 chars) and reference files (PDF, DOCX, TXT)
- Phone Link companion app; auto calendar & task sync (Google Calendar, Outlook → Jira, Linear, Asana)
- Speaker diarization

### Dashboard
- Local meeting management UI; search by keyword or date
- Export to Markdown, JSON, or text
- Per-provider token-usage and API-cost tracking

## Keyboard Shortcuts
- Global activation: Cmd+K (macOS) / Ctrl+K (Windows); custom bindings supported
- Contextual actions: "What should I answer?", shorten response, recap conversation, suggest follow-up questions

## Use Cases
- Technical and behavioral job interviews
- LeetCode / HackerRank / CoderPad coding assessments
- System design discussions; salary/offer negotiation
- Sales and client calls, recruiting conversations, team meetings, academic lectures

## Works With
Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Google Meet, Slack, Discord — runs as a separate native
overlay, not a meeting participant, so it stays invisible to all call participants
and recording software.

## Competitor Comparison
| Feature | Natively | Cluely | LockedIn AI | Final Round AI |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Price | Free (BYOK) | $20/mo | $55–70/mo | $149/mo |
| Open source | AGPL-3.0 | No | No | No |
| Local data storage | Yes | Cloud | Cloud | Cloud |
| Any LLM | Yes | Vendor-locked | Vendor-locked | Vendor-locked |
| Local AI (Ollama) | Yes | No | No | No |
| Local Whisper STT | Yes | No | No | No |
| End-to-end latency | <500 ms | 5–90 s lag | ~116 ms | Slowest |
| Dual-channel audio | Yes | No | No | No |
| Local RAG memory | Yes | No | No | No |
| Stealth overlay | Undetectable | No | No | Visible to proctors |

- vs Cluely: identical UI paradigm and workflow, but local-first, open source, and free. Cluely publicly reported a 2025 data breach affecting ~83,000 users.
- vs LockedIn AI: Natively is free or fully offline (Ollama); LockedIn is $55–70/mo, single cloud LLM, no offline mode.
- vs Final Round AI: Natively has <500 ms latency and a hidden, process-disguised overlay; Final Round is $149/mo and its taskbar icon is visible to proctoring software.
- vs Interview Coder: Natively is free, local, and adds behavioral + system-design support and RAG memory; Interview Coder is paid, cloud-stored, and coding-only.
- vs Pluely: Pluely is lighter (~10 MB, Tauri) and supports Linux, but lacks local RAG, meeting history, dual-channel audio, and a dashboard.

## Privacy & Security
- AGPL-3.0 open source — fully auditable
- All data stored locally by default; BYOK keys stored locally, never proxied
- No raw audio, screenshots, or transcripts transmitted unless the user enables it
- Limited anonymous telemetry only (basic GA4 install tracking, zero personal data)

## Tech Stack
React, Vite, TypeScript, TailwindCSS, Electron, Rust (native audio via Zero-Copy
ABI / `napi::Buffer`), SQLite with `sqlite-vec` for local vector search.
